hierarchy.title: RESERVE BANK OF INDIA
hierarchy.text:
RESERVE BANK OF INDIA
www.rbi.org.in
RBI/2021-22/78


FMRD.DIRD.01/14.01.001/2021-22 April 01, 2021 (Updated as on June 08, 2023) (Updated as on June 25, 2021)

To,

All Eligible Market Participants

Madam/Sir,
Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Call, Notice and Term Money
Markets) Directions, 2021

Please refer to Paragraph 6 of the Statement on Developmental and Regulatory
Policies, Reserve Bank of India, issued as part of the second Bi-monthly Monetary
Policy Statement for 2019-20 dated June 06, 2019 regarding Comprehensive Review
of Money Market Directions.

hierarchy.title: 2. The draft Directions were released for public comments on December 04, 2020.
hierarchy.text:
2. The draft Directions were released for public comments on December 04, 2020.
Based on the feedback received from the market participants, the Reserve Bank of
India (Call, Notice and Term Money Markets) Directions, 2021 were reviewed and
have since been finalised. The Directions are enclosed herewith.
Yours faithfully,
(Dimple Bhandia) Chief General Manager

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
FINANCIAL MARKETS REGULATION DEPARTMENT Notification No.FMRD.DIRD.02/14.01.001/2021-22 dated April 01, 2021
Master Direction- Reserve Bank of India (Call, Notice and Term Money Markets)
Directions, 2021
In exercise of the powers conferred under section 45W of the Reserve Bank of India
Act, 1934 (hereinafter called the Act) read with section 45U of the Act and of all the
powers enabling it in this behalf and in supersession of Section I of the FMRD Master
Direction No.
2/2016-17 dated July 07, 2016, Direction No.
FMRD.DIRD.09/14.01.001/2018-19 dated October 29, 2018 and Direction No.
FMRD.DIRD.01/14.01.001/2020-21 dated December 04, 2020, the Reserve Bank of
India (hereinafter called the Reserve Bank), hereby issues the following Directions to
all persons and agencies eligible to deal in Call, Notice and Term Money Markets.

children.title: (a) These Directions shall be called the Master Direction- Reserve Bank of India
children.text:
(a) These Directions shall be called the Master Direction- Reserve Bank of India
(Call, Notice and Term Money Markets) Directions, 2021.

children.title: (b) These Directions shall come into force with effect from April 05, 2021.
children.text: (b) These Directions shall come into force with effect from April 05, 2021.

children.title: (a) For the purpose of these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires:
children.text: (a) For the purpose of these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires:

children.title: (i) "Bank" means a banking company (including a Payment Bank and a
children.text:
(i) "Bank" means a banking company (including a Payment Bank and a
Small Finance Bank) as defined in clause (c) of section 5 of the Banking
Regulation Act, 1949 or a "regional rural bank", a "corresponding new
bank" or "State Bank of India" as defined in clauses (ja), (da) and (nc),
of section 5 respectively thereof, or a "cooperative bank" as defined in
clause (cci) of section 5 read with section 56 of the said Act;

children.title: (ii) "Call Money" means borrowing or lending in unsecured funds on
children.text:
(ii) "Call Money" means borrowing or lending in unsecured funds on
overnight basis;

children.title: (iii) "Capital Funds" shall have the meaning assigned in the applicable
children.text:
(iii) "Capital Funds" shall have the meaning assigned in the applicable
capital regulations issued by the Department of Regulation of the

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
Reserve Bank as amended from time to time and shall be calculated
as per the latest audited balance sheet;

children.title: (iv) "Electronic Trading Platform" or "ETP" shall have the meaning
children.text:
(iv) "Electronic Trading Platform" or "ETP" shall have the meaning
assigned in paragraph 2 (1) (iii) of the Electronic Trading Platform
(Reserve Bank) Directions, 2018 dated October 05, 2018, as modified
from time to time;

children.title: (v) "Exchange" shall mean 'recognised stock exchange' and shall have
children.text:
(v) "Exchange" shall mean 'recognised stock exchange' and shall have
the same meaning as assigned to in Section 2 (f) of the Securities
Contract Regulation Act, 1956.

children.title: (vi) "Fortnight" shall have the meaning assigned to it under section 42 of
children.text:
(vi) "Fortnight" shall have the meaning assigned to it under section 42 of
the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934;

children.title: (vii) "Negotiated Dealing System-CALL" or "NDS-CALL" is the
(viii) "Net Owned Fund" shall have the meaning assigned to it under the
children.text: (viii) "Net Owned Fund" shall have the meaning assigned to it under the

children.title: Explanation to section 45-IA of the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934;
children.text: Explanation to section 45-IA of the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934;

children.title: (ix) "Notice Money" means borrowing or lending in unsecured funds for
(x) "Over-the-Counter markets" or "OTC markets" refers to markets
children.text:
(x) "Over-the-Counter markets" or "OTC markets" refers to markets
where transactions are undertaken in any manner other than on
exchanges and shall include those executed on electronic trading
platforms;

children.title: (xi) "Payment Bank" means a bank licensed under section 22 of the
children.text:
(xi) "Payment Bank" means a bank licensed under section 22 of the
Banking Regulation Act, 1949 and governed by the terms of the "Reserve
Bank Guidelines for Licensing of Payments Banks" dated November 27,
2014, as amended from time to time;

children.title: (xii) "Primary Dealer" means a Non-Banking Financial Company that holds
children.text:
(xii) "Primary Dealer" means a Non-Banking Financial Company that holds
a letter of authorisation issued by the Reserve Bank to act as a Primary
Dealer, in terms of the "Guidelines for Primary Dealer in Government
Securities Market" dated March 29, 1995, as amended from time to time;

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (xiii) "Small Finance Bank" means a bank licensed under section 22 of the
children.text:
(xiii) "Small Finance Bank" means a bank licensed under section 22 of the
Banking Regulation Act, 1949 and governed by the terms of the "Reserve
Bank Guidelines for Licensing of Small Finance Banks" dated November
27, 2014, as amended from time to time;

children.title: (xiv) "Term Money" means borrowing or lending in unsecured funds for
children.text:
(xiv) "Term Money" means borrowing or lending in unsecured funds for
periods exceeding 14 days and up to one year.

children.title: (b) Words and expressions used but not defined in these Directions shall have the
children.text:
(b) Words and expressions used but not defined in these Directions shall have the
meaning assigned to them in the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

hierarchy.title: 3. Participants
hierarchy.text:
3. Participants
The following entities shall be eligible to participate in the Call, Notice and Term
Money Markets, both as borrowers and lenders:

children.title: (a) Scheduled Commercial Banks (excluding Local Area Banks);
children.text: (a) Scheduled Commercial Banks (excluding Local Area Banks);

children.title: (b) Payment Banks;
children.text: (b) Payment Banks;

children.title: (c) Small Finance Banks;
children.text: (c) Small Finance Banks;

children.title: (d) Regional Rural Banks;
children.text: (d) Regional Rural Banks;

children.title: (e) State Co-operative Banks, District Central Co-operative Banks and Urban Co-
children.text:
(e) State Co-operative Banks, District Central Co-operative Banks and Urban Co-
operative Banks (hereinafter Co-operative Banks); and

children.title: (f) Primary Dealers.
children.text: (f) Primary Dealers.

children.title: (a) Prudential limits in respect of outstanding lending transactions in the Call,
children.text:
(a) Prudential limits in respect of outstanding lending transactions in the Call,
Notice and Term Money Markets shall be decided by the participants with the
approval of their Board within the regulatory framework of the exposure norms
prescribed by the Department of Regulation of the Reserve Bank for the eligible
participant concerned.

children.title: (b) Prudential limits for outstanding borrowing transactions in the Call, Notice and
children.text:
(b) Prudential limits for outstanding borrowing transactions in the Call, Notice and
Term Money Markets are set out in Table 1.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: Table 1: Prudential limits for outstanding borrowing transactions in Call,
children.text:
Table 1: Prudential limits for outstanding borrowing transactions in Call,
Notice and Term Money Markets
Sr. No.
Participant Category Prudential Limit
1 Scheduled Commercial
Call, Notice and Term Money: Internal board
Banks1
approved limits within the prudential limits for inter-
bank liabilities prescribed by Department of
Regulation.
2 Small Finance Banks Call and Notice Money:

children.title: (i) 100% of capital funds, on a daily average basis
children.text:
(i) 100% of capital funds, on a daily average basis
in a reporting fortnight, and

children.title: (ii) 125% of capital funds on any given day.
children.text:
(ii) 125% of capital funds on any given day.
Term Money:

children.title: (i) Internal board approved limit within the
children.text:
(i) Internal board approved limit within the
prudential limits for inter-bank liabilities.
Call, Notice and Term Money:
3 Payment Banks, and
Regional Rural Banks

children.title: (i) 100% of capital funds, on a daily average
children.text:
(i) 100% of capital funds, on a daily average
basis in a reporting fortnight, and

children.title: (ii) 125% of capital funds on any given day.
children.text:
(ii) 125% of capital funds on any given day.
4 Co-operative Banks Call, Notice and Term Money:

children.title: (i) 2.0% of aggregate deposits as at the end of
children.text:
(i) 2.0% of aggregate deposits as at the end of
the previous financial year.
5 Primary Dealers Call and Notice Money:

children.title: (i) 225% of Net Owned Fund (NOF) as at the
children.text:
(i) 225% of Net Owned Fund (NOF) as at the
end of the previous financial year on a daily
average basis in a reporting fortnight.
Term Money2:

children.title: (i) 225% of Net Owned Fund (NOF) as at the
children.text:
(i) 225% of Net Owned Fund (NOF) as at the
end of previous financial year.
1 Inserted vide Circular FMRD.DIRD.02/14.01.001/2023-24 dated June 08, 2023. Prior to the amendment, the prudential limits for Scheduled Commercial Banks (including Small Finance Banks) for Call and Notice Money were (i) 100% of capital funds, on a daily average basis in a reporting fortnight, and (ii) 125% of capital funds on any given day.
2 Inserted vide Circular FMRD.DIRD. 06/14.01.001/2021-22 dated June 25, 2021.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (c) Eligible participants may, with the approval of their respective Board of Directors
children.text:
(c) Eligible participants may, with the approval of their respective Board of Directors
(or equivalent bodies), fix separate internal limits within the prudential limits for
borrowing and lending in the Call, Notice and Term Money Markets. The internal
limits so arrived at by the eligible participants shall be conveyed to the Clearcorp
Dealing System Ltd., or any other NDS-CALL system operator authorised by the
Reserve Bank for setting of limits in the NDS-CALL platform, under advice to the
Financial Markets Regulation Department (FMRD) of the Reserve Bank through
e-mail.

children.title: (a) Interest rates: Eligible participants are free to decide on interest rates in the Call,
children.text:
(a) Interest rates: Eligible participants are free to decide on interest rates in the Call,
Notice and Term Money Markets.

children.title: (b) Trading venues: Call, Notice and Term Money transactions shall be executed
children.text:
(b) Trading venues: Call, Notice and Term Money transactions shall be executed
in Over-the-Counter markets, including on the NDS-CALL platform or any other
Electronic Trading Platform authorised for the purpose by the Reserve Bank.

children.title: (c) Market timings: The market timings for Call, Notice and Term Money
children.text:
(c) Market timings: The market timings for Call, Notice and Term Money
transactions shall be from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM on each business day or as
specified by the Reserve Bank from time to time.

children.title: (d) Market practices and documentation: Eligible participants shall follow the
children.text:
(d) Market practices and documentation: Eligible participants shall follow the
standard market practices, methodologies and documentation prescribed by
Fixed Income Money Market and Derivatives Association of India (FIMMDA), in
consultation with the Reserve Bank, from time to time.

children.title: (a) A Call, Notice or Term Money transaction shall, normally, not be cancelled.
children.text: (a) A Call, Notice or Term Money transaction shall, normally, not be cancelled.

children.title: (b) A Notice or Term Money transaction can be terminated before maturity at a
children.text:
(b) A Notice or Term Money transaction can be terminated before maturity at a
mutually agreed price.

children.title: (c) Any cancellation or termination of a Call, Notice or Term Money transaction
children.text:
(c) Any cancellation or termination of a Call, Notice or Term Money transaction
shall be reported as set out in paragraph 7 of these Directions.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (a) All Call, Notice or Term Money transactions, other than those executed on NDS-
children.text:
(a) All Call, Notice or Term Money transactions, other than those executed on NDS-
CALL platform, shall be reported to the NDS-CALL platform within 15 minutes
of execution (the time when interest rate is agreed), by both counterparties to
the transaction or by the Electronic Trading Platform concerned, as the case
may be. For this purpose, all eligible participants in the Call, Notice and Term
Money Markets shall obtain membership of NDS-CALL platform. Eligible
participants who are not members of NDS-CALL platform shall obtain such
membership within a period of six months from the date of these Directions.

children.title: (b) A Call, Notice or Term Money transaction executed on the NDS-CALL platform
children.text:
(b) A Call, Notice or Term Money transaction executed on the NDS-CALL platform
need not be reported separately.

children.title: (c) Any cancellation or termination of a Call, Notice and Term Money transaction
children.text:
(c) Any cancellation or termination of a Call, Notice and Term Money transaction
shall be reported on the NDS-CALL platform within 15 minutes of cancellation
by each counterparty to the transaction or by the Electronic Trading Platform
concerned, as the case may be.

children.title: (d) Any misreporting or multiple reporting of the same OTC markets deal by a
children.text:
(d) Any misreporting or multiple reporting of the same OTC markets deal by a
counterparty shall be immediately brought to the notice of the Clearcorp Dealing
System Ltd., or any other NDS-CALL system operator authorised by the
Reserve Bank and also to the Financial Markets Regulation Department,
Reserve Bank of India, Central Office, Fort, Mumbai, through email.

hierarchy.title: 8. Obligation to provide information sought by the Reserve Bank: The Reserve
hierarchy.text:
8. Obligation to provide information sought by the Reserve Bank: The Reserve
Bank may call for any information or statement or seek any clarification, which in the
opinion of the Reserve Bank is relevant, from persons or agencies dealing in the Call,
Notice and Term Money Markets, including eligible participants, and such persons,
agencies and participants shall furnish such information, statement or clarification.

hierarchy.title: 9. Dissemination of data: The Reserve Bank or any other person authorised by the
hierarchy.text:
9. Dissemination of data: The Reserve Bank or any other person authorised by the
Reserve Bank, may publish any anonymised data related to transactions in Call,
Notice and Term Money Markets.

hierarchy.title: 10. Violation of Directions: In the event of any person or agency violating any
hierarchy.text:
10. Violation of Directions: In the event of any person or agency violating any
provision of these Directions or the provisions of any other applicable law, the Reserve
Bank may, in addition to taking any penal or regulatory action in accordance with
law, disallow that person or agency from dealing in the Call, Notice and Term Money

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
Markets for a period not exceeding one month at a time, after providing reasonable
opportunity to the person or agency to defend its actions, and such action may be
made public by the Reserve Bank.

hierarchy.title: 11. These Directions shall apply to Call, Notice and Term Money transactions entered
hierarchy.text:
11. These Directions shall apply to Call, Notice and Term Money transactions entered
into from the date these Directions come into force. Provisions of Section I of the
FMRD Master Direction No. 2/2016-17 dated July 07, 2016; Direction No.
FMRD.DIRD.09/14.01.001/2018-19 dated October 29, 2018 and Direction No.
FMRD.DIRD.01/14.01.001/2020-21 dated December 04, 2020, shall continue to be
applicable to transactions undertaken in accordance with the said Directions till the
expiry of those contracts.
